local_names.romanized_local: Suan Mian Bao (Shou Gong Chang Fa Jiao)

cuisine: Western / Artisan Bakery — Singapore café culture
region_of_origin: Ancient fermentation-leavened bread tradition; modern artisan sourdough revival originating in San Francisco (~1800s), now established in Singapore artisan bakery scene (Tiong Bahru Bakery est. 2012, Starter Lab est. 2015)
food_subgroup: Long-fermented wheat sourdough bread; leavened with live wild-yeast and Lactobacillus starter culture; GI ~55 (lower than commercial white bread ~71) due to lactic and acetic acid produced during ≥8-hour fermentation

ontology.foodex2_description: Sourdough bread (wheat, long-fermented with Lactobacillus starter)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00HK is the closest parent for sourdough wheat bread; no dedicated long-fermented artisan sourdough sub-code exists in the public FoodEx2 catalogue.

main_ingredients: sourdough starter (wild yeast and Lactobacillus bacteria)

serving.description: 1 thick slice (~80g) — Singapore artisan bakery standard
serving.natural_language_portion: One thick slice of artisan sourdough bread weighing approximately 80g, consistent with the standard portion served at Singapore artisan bakeries such as Starter Lab and Tiong Bahru Bakery.

nutrient_source.title: Sourdough Bread — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172686)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172686 is a direct match for sourdough bread. All per-100g nutrient values are sourced from this entry. GI value sourced separately from the University of Sydney GI Database (Brand-Miller et al.; sourdough wheat bread, long-fermented).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — sourdough wheat bread, long-fermented (Brand-Miller et al.)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~55 for long-fermented sourdough wheat bread is directly referenced in the University of Sydney GI Database (Brand-Miller et al.). GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 49.8 × 0.80) / 100 = (55 × 39.84) / 100 ≈ 21.9; input record states 20.6 using slightly rounded carbohydrate per serving (~37.5g net), consistent with the source. Value 20.6 preserved from verified input. CRITICAL NOTE: This GI applies ONLY to authentic long-fermented artisan sourdough. Commercial sourdough-style breads (e.g., Gardenia, BreadTalk) made with yeast and added acids have GI ~67–72 and do not qualify.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content — 450mg per 100g; ~360mg per 80g slice (~18% of WHO 2,000mg/day limit per slice)
hypertension.guidance: One thick slice (80g) contributes approximately 360mg sodium (~18% of the WHO daily limit); individuals with hypertension should limit to one slice per meal and monitor total daily sodium from all bread and processed food sources, as two slices already provide ~720mg sodium (~36% of the daily limit).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~55 (low) — significantly lower than commercial white bread (~71) due to lactic and acetic acid produced during long fermentation, which slows starch digestion; GL ~20.6 per slice remains moderate
type2_diabetes.guidance: Authentic long-fermented artisan sourdough (GI ~55) is a clinically preferable bread choice for T2DM management compared to white bread (GI ~71), but the GL of ~20.6 per 80g slice is still moderate and portion control remains essential; commercial sourdough-style breads made with added acids do not achieve GI ~55 and should not be substituted.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.4g/100g), zero trans fat, moderate dietary fibre (3.1g/100g), and meaningful folate (70mcg/100g); sodium is the primary cardiovascular concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Artisan sourdough bread is low in saturated fat and trans fat, making it a heart-friendly bread choice; however, the sodium content of ~360mg per slice warrants attention for individuals managing cardiovascular risk, and total daily sodium from all sources should be tracked to remain within the WHO 2,000mg/day guideline.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low fat, meaningful selenium (~22mcg per slice) and folate (~56mcg per slice); long fermentation partially breaks down phytic acid, improving iron and zinc bioavailability
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Artisan sourdough is a suitable bread for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; the long fermentation process improves iron and zinc bioavailability by reducing phytic acid, and the selenium content supports immune function, though it is not a primary therapeutic food.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Artisan long-fermented sourdough bread is a naturally leavened wheat bread made with a live culture of wild yeast and Lactobacillus bacteria (sourdough starter), water, wheat flour, and salt — no commercial yeast or additives. The defining feature is the long fermentation period of ≥8 hours, during which lactic and acetic acids are produced. These organic acids slow starch digestion and lower the glycemic index to approximately 55 — significantly lower than commercial white bread (GI ~71) and commercial sourdough-style bread (GI ~67–72). The bread has a characteristic tangy flavour, chewy crumb, and thick crust. Per 1 thick slice (~80g, Singapore artisan bakery standard): approximately 208 kcal, 7.0g protein, 1.5g fat, 39.8g carbohydrate, 2.5g dietary fibre, GI ~55, GL ~20.6, ~360mg sodium, ~56mcg folate, ~22mcg selenium. In Singapore, authentic long-fermented artisan sourdough is available at Starter Lab (est. 2015), Tiong Bahru Bakery (est. 2012), and Craftsmen Specialty Coffee. CRITICAL CLINICAL NOTE: The GI benefit is PROCESS-DEPENDENT — only authentic long-fermented sourdough achieves GI ~55. Commercial sourdough-style breads (Gardenia, BreadTalk) made with yeast and vinegar or citric acid do NOT achieve this GI and should not be recommended as a lower-GI alternative for diabetes management. Nutrient data sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172686; GI from University of Sydney GI Database (Brand-Miller et al.).
common_user_queries: is sourdough better for blood sugar

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172686 (exact match for sourdough bread). GI value of 55 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database (Brand-Miller et al.) for long-fermented sourdough wheat bread. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy. Input record confidence was 1.0 with verified provenance.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for sourdough bread not separately reported in USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172686 at the level required; these values are not available from the primary approved source. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy entry used. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 172686 — plain wheat sourdough bread contains no hydrogenated fats). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — sourdough bread is a plant-based product containing no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0, vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0, vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0, vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero or negligible per USDA SR Legacy — plain wheat bread contains no significant amounts of these nutrients). GI ~55 applies ONLY to authentic long-fermented artisan sourdough; commercial sourdough-style breads have GI ~67–72. GL per serving (20.6) is calculated at 80g serving size. Sodium per 80g slice is approximately 360mg (~18% of WHO 2,000mg/day limit). FoodEx2 code A00HK is the closest parent; no dedicated sub-code for long-fermented artisan sourdough exists in the public FoodEx2 catalogue.
